text: It is late and the building is quiet, almost eerily so. Shadows press against the glass walls and the sterile light of a security lamp gives an artificial sense of order. The second room, with its letters 'R+D' and closed computers, hints at work that pauses but never ends—resting in darkness. I feel a sense of curiosity mixed with solitude, as if I’m witnessing the world after industry sleeps, and pondering what sparks innovation when lights are off.
